candidate code. A GitHub-generated source archive is treated as untrusted
data inspected by the base branch's checker. Keep permissions read-only and
add no repository or environment secrets.

title: Blocking bootstrap baseline: validate that the

headings.text: invariant -- see docs/INVARIANTS.md). Split out of ci.yml (D-0314) so its
10+ minute two-clean-build cost only lands on PRs that could plausibly
change a build artifact. `push: branches: [main]` has no path filter --
every commit that reaches main is checked, unconditionally, matching
The `pull_request` trigger has no path filter either (D-0325): an earlier
version used `paths-ignore: [docs/**, **/*.md]` to skip the workflow
entirely for docs-only PRs, but that meant this job -- listed as a
required status check -- never posted *any* status for such a PR, which
left it permanently stuck at `mergeable_state: blocked` no matter how long
CI ran (exactly the risk D-0320's own follow-up section flagged and never
resolved). The workflow itself now always runs and always completes with
a real conclusion; the "Determine whether this PR touches only docs" step
below decides, from a real `git diff` against the PR's base commit,
whether the two-clean-build steps that follow are skipped. A required
check that sometimes finishes in five seconds is still a required check
that always finishes -- unlike one that never starts.

headings.text: Mininet — Cargo workspace for the self-contained Rust core.
Founder Decision A1 now targets a custom Rust chain, not Cosmos/Go. Identity
and presence remain ledger-independent (SPEC-01 G8), so this workspace can
build the two-phone Bluetooth demo before the chain lands.
Deterministic codegen helps us toward SPEC-11's frozen "verified reproducible"
release requirement. (Full hermetic/reproducible builds are a later batch; this
is the cheap, non-surprising part.) panic strategy is left at the default so

headings.text: Before anything else: read the honest limits
What this document is, and is not
3. Identity: the phone (or device) as sovereign root
4. Money: signed promises, not custodial balances
5. Privacy is a purchasable resource, never a purchasable right
7. What is honestly not solved yet
8. Where the design is heading: a public information commons
9. Design directions under active discussion (not yet decided)
Identity builds only on the crypto foundation. Keeping the dependency surface
this small is deliberate: this crate is security-critical and must stay easy to
review and reproduce (SPEC-11). It has NO network or chain dependency — SPEC-01
G8: identity is verifiable peer-to-peer, the chain is only an optional anchor.
Tests reconstruct expected keys directly from seeds.

headings.text: Exact patch versions pinned deliberately (D-0069 dependency-governance
requirement): this is the one crate in the tree where an unreviewed
transitive upgrade is a real security event, not routine maintenance.
Never `cargo update -p wasmtime` on autopilot -- bump in a reviewed
commit that re-runs the Batch 2b.3 adversarial suite.
Bumped 27.0.0 -> 46.0.1 (2026-07-10, same PR, before merge): the
dependency-audit CI job flagged that 27.0.0 carries multiple open
RustSec advisories against wasmtime-wasi specifically, including two
that bypass the exact boundary this crate exists to enforce --
and GHSA-4ch3-9j33-3pmj (hard links/renames bypass `FilePerms` for the
destination) -- plus a long list of Wasmtime-core traps/panics/
46.0.1 is the first version with all of them fixed; see this crate's
README for what changed in the WASI Preview 2 API surface across the
`insecure_random` require (`wasmtime_wasi::Rng` is a re-export of the
same trait). Version pinned to match what wasmtime-wasi 46.0.1 itself
resolves to, so there is exactly one `rand_core` in the dependency
graph, not two incompatible major versions.
Only needed to mint a validly-signed `ObjectId` for the Batch 2b.3
never a dependency of this crate's actual runner logic.

headings.text: What this crate does not claim
Deliberately none: this crate is pure typed policy data plus a hand-rolled
wire codec, matching this workspace's no-serde convention. No AEAD, relay,
or mixnet mechanism is implemented here yet -- see the crate-level docs.

title: Deliberately none: this crate is pure typed policy data plus a hand-rolled

headings.text: Only mini-privacy-policy -- deliberately no dependency on mini-value or
mini-treasury: this crate follows the plain "amount_micro: u64" (micro-MINI)
rather than importing a type, keeping this crate's own dependency graph
minimal. And deliberately, permanently, no dependency on mini-forge or
mini-chain -- pricing a resource must never become a governance signal
(Directive 1, the voice/value wall; CLAUDE.md's hard rule).

title: Only mini-privacy-policy -- deliberately no dependency on mini-value or

headings.text: The only dependency: raw signing/verification and hashing. This crate is
deliberately decoupled from mini-value's stealth/ring modules and from
did-mini's KEL layer -- a payer/payee "authority" here is just a public
key, so callers choose what kind of key it is (a stealth one-time key,
a did:mini device key, whatever fits) without this crate taking a
position on addressing or anonymity. Its only job is the settlement
state machine and reconciliation rules M1/M2/M3 require.

headings.text: mini-build-runner-wasmtime, the first crate in this workspace to pull in
a dependency tree large enough -- Wasmtime plus its own transitive
graph -- that license/advisory drift stops being something a human can
eyeball in a `cargo tree` diff). Scoped to the whole workspace, not just
that one crate, since cargo-deny operates on the resolved dependency
Honesty note: the `cargo-deny` binary is not available in the sandbox
this file was authored in, so this configuration has not been run
locally -- it is written from the licenses/advisories actually declared
in `cargo tree`'s output for this workspace's dependencies, not
verified end-to-end. The CI job wiring this in (`.github/workflows/
ci.yml`) runs `continue-on-error: true` for the same reason the
`dependency-audit` job does: until a first real CI run establishes a
baseline, a false-positive failure here must not block merges.
Every license actually in use across this workspace's dependency graph
at the time of writing (mini-crypto/did-mini's curve25519-dalek stack,
mini-value's Bulletproofs machinery, and now Wasmtime's own tree via
mini-build-runner-wasmtime). CC0-1.0 is this workspace's own license

headings.text: Scope rule (added D-0045 batch, per founder review)
Decision-number allocation across parallel tracks (added D-0200 batch)
D-0001 — On-device core language: Rust; single-language stack  ·  *Accepted*
D-0003 — Signature suite: Ed25519 default, behind a versioned tag  ·  *Provisional*
D-0004 — Content-address hash: BLAKE3 default, SHA-256 interop, SHA-1 forbidden  ·  *Accepted (freeze)*
D-0005 — Identity foundation: KERI-style autonomic identifiers  ·  *Accepted*
D-0006 — Reproducibility hygiene: commit `Cargo.lock`, pin the toolchain  ·  *Accepted*
D-0007 — did:mini wire profile: hand-rolled deterministic codec; BLAKE3 SAID-style SCID  ·  *Accepted*
D-0008 — Chain framework (A1): custom Rust chain on an adapted proven BFT  ·  *Accepted*
D-0009 — Networking core (A3): mesh + local Wi-Fi + optional relay; drop radio  ·  *Accepted*
D-0010 — Device delegation model (M2): delegated inception + mutual seal  ·  *Accepted*
D-0011 — Self-contained genesis and self-updating release registry  ·  *Accepted*
D-0012 — Bluetooth-only bootstrap is a mandatory launch path  ·  *Accepted*
D-0013 — did:mini wire hardening before first public release  ·  *Accepted*
D-0014 — Pack 1 crypto primitives for Bluetooth/local encrypted sessions  ·  *Accepted*
D-0015 — Bearer layer and anonymous encrypted channel (`mini-bearer`)  ·  *Accepted*
D-0016 — Presence attestations (`mini-presence`): range-bound, mutually signed  ·  *Accepted*
D-0017 — Reward accrual stub (`mini-reward`): slow, diversity-weighted vesting toward MINI  ·  *Accepted, corrected 2026-07-08*
D-0018 — Keystone harness (`mini-keystone`): the beta is the composed flow  ·  *Accepted*
D-0019 — UI beta stack: one Rust core, Flutter shell, WASM web, SPEC-09 objects  ·  *Proposed (founder veto open)*
D-0020 — Sovereignty-first UI & distribution stack  ·  *Accepted (founder directive)*
D-0028 — Merge governance & self-amending maintainer chain (`mini-forge::governance`)  ·  *Accepted*
D-0029 — External review integrated: hardening pass (Batches 7A/7B/7C-core)  ·  *Accepted*
D-0030 — Second review integrated: compile-readiness + honest quorum semantics  ·  *Accepted*
D-0031 — Third review integrated: truth-sync + root-carrier provenance + repo self-description  ·  *Accepted*
D-0032 — Parser strictness, artifact-only verifier rename, KEL cache caps, and repo-map restoration  ·  *Accepted*
D-0033 — Founder decisions batch: public walls, base device, seed-on-view, 2-approval floor, radio/Cosmos closed for good  ·  *Accepted*
D-0034 — Ranging (UWB), uniqueness graph, P2P networking (libp2p), and privacy-preserving value transfer: four founder decisions, phased  ·  *Accepted (founder directive, phased implementation)*

D-0035 — Whitepaper reconciliation: MINI unification, three-signal personhood, hybrid consensus, human-only crypto core — and one open contradiction  ·  *Accepted where noted; one item explicitly OPEN*
D-0036 — Founder override: AI-authored ring-signature/stealth-address prototype for `mini-value`, ahead of external audit  ·  *Accepted (explicit founder override of D-0035 point 5)*
D-0037 — Founder policy change: D-0035 point 5 generalized to "human review, AI authorship permitted"  ·  *Accepted (supersedes D-0036's narrow scope)*
D-0038 — Founder redesign: open-ended, multi-signal `HumanStatus` accumulator, replacing reliance on any single silver-bullet signal  ·  *Accepted*
D-0039 — `mini-spacetime`: Merkle/PDP storage proof implemented as the honest interim scheme  ·  *Accepted*
D-0040 — `mini-value`: Bulletproofs range proof implemented for confidential amounts  ·  *Accepted*
D-0041 — `mini-treasury`: FROST threshold-signature custody + live multi-device demo  ·  *Accepted*
D-0042 — `mini-bearer`: real TCP transport (`TcpBearer`) + a live multi-process gossip demo in `mini-net`  ·  *Accepted*
D-0043 — `docs/FOUNDER_DIRECTIVES.md` adopted as a canonical document  ·  *Accepted*
D-0044 — Master roadmap opened; first four audit issues closed; CI gained real dependency scanning and reproducibility checks  ·  *Accepted*
D-0045 — Canonical money finality during outages  ·  *Accepted*
D-0051 — Bounty & review system: money funds work, never a decision  ·  *Accepted*
D-0052 — Fork legitimacy: four checkable continuity criteria  ·  *Accepted*
D-0053 — Identity audit hardening: recovery, threshold policy, delegation-chain refusal  ·  *Accepted*
D-0054 — Personhood promotion requires a live seed-anchored signal  ·  *Accepted*
D-0055 — `mini-settlement`: offline transaction settlement, implementing M1/M2/M3  ·  *Accepted*
D-0056 — External Legitimacy Gates: naming what more code cannot close  ·  *Accepted*
D-0057 — README as a human trust gateway; audience-door docs  ·  *Accepted*
D-0058 — `mini-settlement`: rename `nonce` field to `sequence` (terminology correction, not a design change)  ·  *Accepted*
D-0059 — `mini-treasury`: zeroize FROST nonces on drop, gate trusted-dealer keygen behind an explicit acknowledgment  ·  *Accepted*
D-0060 — `mini-treasury`: real FROST DKG (Pedersen) and committee resharing, closing D-0048's remaining implementation gap  ·  *Accepted*
D-0061 — `mini-execution`: a real, chain-backed `CanonicalLedgerView`, closing roadmap #40  ·  *Accepted*
D-0062 — `mini-bootstrap`/`mini-sync`: proven live over real TCP, closing roadmap #23  ·  *Accepted*
D-0063 — Clarify "no new cryptographic primitives": published, real-world-proven constructions are composition, not invention  ·  *Accepted*
D-0064 — `mini-porep`: real proof-of-replication (Stacked Depth-Robust Graph sealing), closes roadmap #31  ·  *Accepted*
D-0065 — `mini-erasure`: systematic Reed-Solomon erasure coding + self-healing repair, closes roadmap #30 and #32  ·  *Accepted*
D-0066 — Adopt external audit's sequencing: pause horizontal crate breadth, build the vertical self-hosted developer spine first  ·  *Accepted*
D-0067 — `mini-cli` + `mini-forge` review metadata: Batch 1's developer spine, first exit-condition demonstration  ·  *Accepted*
D-0068 — `mini-provenance`: build provenance as signed objects (Batch 2a); Batch 2b (WASI/Wasmtime sandbox) deferred pending an explicit dependency decision  ·  *Accepted*
D-0069 — Adopt Wasmtime as the reference executor for untrusted pipeline components, isolated to a dedicated runner  ·  *Accepted*
D-0070 — Ship self-hosted forge spine Batch 3: TUF-adapted release verification (rollback protection, transparency log, freshness, provenance quorum)  ·  *Accepted*
D-0071 — Ship self-hosted forge spine Batch 4: real installation, `mini-installer`  ·  *Accepted*
D-0072 — Fix `mini-erasure`'s generator matrix: naive Vandermonde-append did not have the promised MDS property  ·  *Accepted*
D-0073 — Treasury economic model: XRPL/XMR two-bridge design replaces BTC/XMR framing  ·  *Accepted*
D-0074 — Long-term issuance envelope and formal anti-whale wall  ·  *Accepted*
D-0075 — Private Human Continuity Proof redefines personhood signal (b)  ·  *Accepted*

headings.text: Group 1: Identity & devices (`did-mini`, self-certifying identifiers, delegation, pre-rotation)
Group 2: Personhood & Sybil resistance (`mini-uniqueness`, presence, vouching graph) — **the sharpest "maybe" in this review**
Group 4: Consensus & chain (`mini-chain`, validator sets, finality)
Group 7: AI's role (governance recommendations, code authorship, moderation)
What was checked and found correct
F1 (fixed) — Rotation silently rewrote the M-of-N policy to N-of-N
F2 (fixed) — `verify_delegation` accepted a delegated identity as a root
F3 (fixed) — Unscrubbed seed copies in pairwise-pseudonym derivation
F4 (open, launch-blocking for anything that needs revocation or recency) — No KEL freshness/duplicity layer
F5 (recorded, no action) — Century-scale KEL growth cap
F6 (recorded, no action) — Assumptions inherited from mini-crypto
Device-compromise summary (what a thief of one device gets)
The recovery model that now exists
Bugs found and fixed during this audit
Relay attack (attacker relays between two distant real devices) — ⚠ NOT fully defended (known, structural)
Time spoofing (forging `started_at_ms`/`finished_at_ms`) — ✅ defended for freshness, ⚠ inherent for absolute time
GPS spoofing (fake location commitments) — ✅ not trusted (by design)
BLE replay (replaying a captured legitimate exchange) — ✅ defended (multiple independent layers)
Device cloning (same device identity on two physical devices) — ✅ bounded to the same limit as all key custody
Finding F1 (fixed this batch) — Farm-saturation bypass of the honest graph
Long-term & sleeping Sybils — the age gate is a speed bump, not a wall
Nation-state Sybils — not defended, and honestly cannot be by this layer alone
2. `mini_objects::ObjectId` — is the id ever trusted instead of recomputed?
3. `mini_store::Store` — is content-addressing enforced on read, or only assumed?
2. Dependency tree: does `unsafe` appear, and is it expected?
3. Dependency-vulnerability scanning: a real finding from setting this up
Bounty & review system design — money funds work, never a decision

Pinning the toolchain is part of moving toward reproducible builds (SPEC-11).
The exact channel is a TUNABLE detail; pinning *something* is the frozen-spirit
requirement. Bump deliberately, in a reviewed commit, and re-verify builds.
forge spine Batch 2b): Wasmtime's own dependency tree (idna_adapter, via
wasmtime-wasi's networking support) requires Cargo's `edition2024` feature,
stabilized in Rust 1.85. Only mini-build-runner-wasmtime and its two
every other crate in the workspace was re-verified against the newer
clean). The newer clippy added lints the old toolchain didn't have
pre-existing call sites in did-mini, mini-spacetime, and mini-uniqueness
needed small non-behavioral rewrites to satisfy them -- not "unchanged,"
but no crate's logic or test coverage changed, only lint-clean phrasing.

title: Pinning the toolchain is part of moving toward reproducible builds (SPEC-11).

headings.text: One-line, faithful distillations of each directive's own text below —
not new claims, just short enough to scan in a table. The digest field
is what actually binds a registry entry to the canonical prose; this
statement is a human-readable label, not the source of truth.

title: One-line, faithful distillations of each directive's own text below —

headings.text: The no-GitHub outage demo (D-0081, self-hosted forge spine roadmap #102).
GitHub is this project's UAT/mirror, never its source of truth
(CLAUDE.md). This script is the tangible proof: it drives the real,
compiled `mini` binary -- never a library call, never GitHub -- through
the entire developer lifecycle this project's constitution depends on
being able to survive a GitHub outage:
identity -> repo -> commit -> PR -> two independent reviews -> governed
merge -> release -> two independent attestations -> verify -> install
-> passing health check -- and then, because a real system must survive
its own mistakes too, a second, DELIBERATELY BROKEN release through the
identical path -> a failing health check -> automatic rollback -> a
clean, independently-verifiable event log proving exactly what happened.
Every step below is a real `mini` subcommand against real files on real
disk. No network call to any GitHub endpoint exists anywhere in this
codebase to make -- there is nothing to "route around" during an outage,
because nothing here was ever routed through GitHub to begin with. That
is the honest claim this script demonstrates, not a network firewall
drill (this environment has no controlled way to actually sever GitHub
reachability, and simulating a firewall would prove less than reading
this script and the codebase's own dependency graph already does).
Defaults to <repo-root>/target/debug/mini if not given -- override with
an explicit path (e.g. a release build, or $CARGO_BIN_EXE_mini when
--json (D-0078) hands back real structured fields -- release_id and
artifact_digest -- instead of a human sentence to scrape, exactly the
machine-readable contract this demo relies on for the next two steps.

title: The no-GitHub outage demo (D-0081, self-hosted forge spine roadmap #102).
